title: विष्णु स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री विष्णु स्तोत्रम् ॥

नमः समस्तभूतानामादिभूताय भूभृते।
अनेकरूपरूपाय विष्णवे प्रभविष्णवे॥ १॥

नमस्ते देवदेवेश नमस्ते मधुसूदन।
नमस्ते पुण्डरीकाक्ष नमस्ते गरुडध्वज॥ २॥

नमस्ते कमलेशाय नमस्ते जलशायिने।
नमस्ते वासुदेवाय सदा रक्ष जनार्दन॥ ३॥

शङ्खचक्रगदापाणे द्वारकानिलयाच्युत।
गोविन्द पुण्डरीकाक्ष रक्ष मां शरणागतम्॥ ४॥

यद्भयात् संभवत्यग्निर्यद्भयात् तपते रविः।
यद्भयाद्वाति वायुश्च मृत्युर्धावति पञ्चमः।
तस्मै नमो भगवते श्रीविष्णवे परमात्मने॥ ५॥

त्वमादिदेवः पुरुषः पुराणः
त्वमस्य विश्वस्य परं निधानम्।
वेत्तासि वेद्यं च परं च धाम
त्वया ततं विश्वमनन्तरूप॥ ६॥

वायुर्यमोऽग्निर्वरुणः शशाङ्कः
प्रजापतिस्त्वं प्रपितामहश्च।
नमो नमस्तेऽस्तु सहस्रकृत्वः
पुनश्च भूयोऽपि नमो नमस्ते॥ ७॥

नमः पुरस्तादथ पृष्ठतस्ते
नमोऽस्तु ते सर्वत एव सर्व।
अनन्तवीर्यामितविक्रमस्त्वं
सर्वं समाप्नोषि ततोऽसि सर्वः॥ ८॥

श्रीवत्सवक्षा नित्यश्रीः जगन्मोहन चेष्टित।
श्रीमन्नारायणो विष्णुर्वासुदेवोऽभिरक्षतु॥ ९॥

सशङ्खचक्रो विभुर्विष्णुर्दुर्जनभयावहः।
असुरारिर्विभूतीनां सम्पत्करो जनार्दनः॥ १०॥

यतो धर्मस्ततो कृष्णो यतः कृष्णस्ततो जयः।
ततो विष्णुर्जगन्नाथो नमस्तस्मै महात्मने॥ ११॥

सर्वस्य चाहं हृदि सन्निविष्टो
मत्तः स्मृतिर्ज्ञानमपोहनं च।
वेदैश्च सर्वैरहमेव वेद्यो
वेदान्तकृद्वेदविदेव चाहम्॥ १२॥

त्वमेव माता च पिता त्वमेव
त्वमेव बन्धुश्च सखा त्वमेव।
त्वमेव विद्या द्रविणं त्वमेव
त्वमेव सर्वं मम देवदेव॥ १३॥

कायेन वाचा मनसेन्द्रियैर्वा
बुद्ध्यात्मना वा प्रकृतेः स्वभावात्।
करोमि यद्यत् सकलं परस्मै
नारायणायेति समर्पयामि॥ १४॥

सर्वधर्मान्परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज।
अहं त्वां सर्वपापेभ्यो मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः॥ १५॥

ॐ नमो भगवते वासुदेवाय।
ॐ नमो नारायणाय।
ॐ विष्णवे नमः।

॥ इति श्री विष्णु स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Vishnu Stotram ||

Namah samasta-bhutanam-adi-bhutaya bhubhrite |
Aneka-rupa-rupaya Vishnave prabhvishnave || 1 ||

Namaste deva-devesha namaste Madhusudana |
Namaste Pundarikaksha namaste Garudadhvaja || 2 ||

Namaste Kamaleshaya namaste jalashayine |
Namaste Vasudevaya sada raksha Janardana || 3 ||

Shankha-chakra-gada-pane Dvaraka-nilaya-Achyuta |
Govinda Pundarikaksha raksha mam sharanaagatam || 4 ||

Yad-bhayat sambhavaty-agnir-yad-bhayat tapate ravih |
Yad-bhayad-vati vayushcha mrityur-dhavati panchamah |
Tasmai namo Bhagavate Shri-Vishnave Paramatmane || 5 ||

Tvam-adi-devah Purushah Puranah
tvam-asya vishvasya param nidhanam |
Vettasi vedyam cha param cha dhama
tvaya tatam vishvam-ananta-rupa || 6 ||

Vayur-Yamo-Agnir-Varunah shashankah
Prajapatih-tvam prapitamahashcha |
Namo namaste-astu sahasra-kritvah
punashcha bhuyo-api namo namaste || 7 ||

Namah purastad-atha prishthataste
namo-astu te sarvata eva sarva |
Ananta-viryamita-vikramah-tvam
sarvam samapnoshi tato-asi sarvah || 8 ||

Shrivatsa-vaksha nityashrih jagan-mohana cheshtita |
Shriman-Narayano Vishnur-Vasudevo-abhirakshatu || 9 ||

Sashankha-chakro vibhur-Vishnur-durjana-bhayavahah |
Asura-arir-vibhutinam sampatkaro Janardanah || 10 ||

Yato dharmah-tato Krishno yatah Krishnah-tato jayah |
Tato Vishnur-Jagannathah namastasmai Mahatmane || 11 ||

Sarvasya chaham hridi sannivishto
mattah smritir-jnanam-apohanam cha |
Vedaishcha sarvair-aham-eva vedyo
Vedanta-krid-Veda-vid-eva chaham || 12 ||

Tvameva mata cha pita tvameva
tvameva bandhushcha sakha tvameva |
Tvameva vidya dravinam tvameva
tvameva sarvam mama Deva-Deva || 13 ||

Kayena vacha manasendriyair-va
buddhy-atmana va prakriteh svabhavat |
Karomi yad-yat sakalam parasmai
Narayanayeti samarpayami || 14 ||

Sarva-dharman-parityajya mam-ekam sharanam vraja |
Aham tvam sarva-papebhyo mokshayishyami ma shuchah || 15 ||

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya |
Om Namo Narayanaya |
Om Vishnave Namah |

|| Iti Shri Vishnu Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
समस्त प्राणियों के आदि भूत, पृथ्वी को धारण करने वाले, अनेक रूपों वाले विष्णु को, प्रभावशाली को नमस्कार॥ १॥

हे देवों के देवेश! आपको नमस्कार। हे मधुसूदन! आपको नमस्कार। हे पुण्डरीकाक्ष (कमल नेत्र)! आपको नमस्कार। हे गरुड़ध्वज! आपको नमस्कार॥ २॥

कमलों के ईश को नमस्कार, जल पर शयन करने वाले को नमस्कार। वासुदेव को नमस्कार, हे जनार्दन! सदा मेरी रक्षा करें॥ ३॥

हे शंख, चक्र, गदा हाथ में धारण करने वाले! हे द्वारका निवासी अच्युत! हे गोविन्द! हे पुण्डरीकाक्ष! शरणागत मेरी रक्षा करें॥ ४॥

जिनके भय से अग्नि प्रज्वलित होती है, जिनके भय से सूर्य तपता है, जिनके भय से वायु बहती है और मृत्यु पाँचवाँ दौड़ती है — उन भगवान श्रीविष्णु परमात्मा को नमस्कार॥ ५॥

आप आदि देव हैं, पुराण पुरुष हैं, इस विश्व के परम निधान हैं। जानने वाले, जानने योग्य और परम धाम हैं — आपसे यह समस्त विश्व व्याप्त है, हे अनन्त रूप!॥ ६॥

आप ही माता हैं और आप ही पिता हैं, आप ही बन्धु हैं और आप ही सखा हैं। आप ही विद्या हैं और आप ही धन हैं — हे देवदेव! आप ही मेरे सब कुछ हैं॥ १३॥

सब धर्मों को छोड़कर केवल मेरी शरण में आओ। मैं तुम्हें सब पापों से मुक्त कर दूँगा, शोक मत करो॥ १५॥
benefits: Comprehensive prayer invoking all aspects of Lord Vishnu's grace
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of sins and purifies the soul
Traditionally associated with divine protection and blessings
Invokes peace of mind and freedom from fear
Invokes the blessing of wealth, health, and prosperity
Leads to spiritual liberation and moksha
Suitable for daily recitation by all devotees
description: Vishnu Stotram — विष्णु स्तोत्रम् — is a devotional hymn of praise offered to Lord Vishnu. Drawn from Vishnu Purana and Bhagavad Gita (Compilation), this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Devotees recite this prayer to bring divine protection and blessings and remove all sins and purify the soul. It is also said to grant peace of mind and freedom from fear. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Thursday (Guruvar) and Sunday (Ravivar) and during Ekadashi. With 15 verses and a reading time of about 10 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Vishnu Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Vishnu Stotram in Sanskrit with English transliteration and Hindi meaning. Devotional hymn to Lord Vishnu with verses from Gita and Puranas.
relatedSearches: Vishnu twelve names Dwadasa Nama Stotram

seoFaqs.question: Which composition is meant when one refers simply to Vishnu Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The phrase Vishnu Stotram is a generic category covering any praise hymn addressed to Vishnu, and printed manuals therefore vary in what they reproduce under this title. The four most prominent texts that share the bare title Vishnu Stotram are: (1) the Vishnu Sahasranama Stotram from the Anushasana Parva of the Mahabharata, narrated by Bhishma to Yudhishthira; (2) the Bhishma Stuti from Bhagavata Purana 1.9, recited by Bhishma at his death-bed in the presence of Krishna; (3) the Akrurakrta Vishnu Stotram preserved in the Bhagavata Purana 10.40, recited by Akrura when he saw Krishna and Balarama within the Yamuna waters; and (4) the Sanat Kumara Vishnu Stotram from the Padma Purana, taught by the Kumara-rishi Sanat Kumara to Sage Narada. Devotees should clarify which Vishnu Stotram is intended in context. (Sources: Shivalli Brahmins Vishnu Sahasranama; Stotra Nidhi Bhishma Kruta Bhagavat Stuti; StotraSamhita Akrurakrta Vishnu Stotram.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Bhishma Vishnu Stuti from the Bhagavata Purana?
seoFaqs.answer: The Bhishma Stuti is one of the most cherished Vishnu hymns in the Bhagavata Purana corpus, occupying chapter 9 of the first canto. As the Pandavas and Krishna gather at Bhishma's deathbed on the bed-of-arrows after the Mahabharata war, Bhishma fixes his gaze on Krishna and recites a heartfelt praise hymn invoking him as the supreme purusha, the one who incarnates for dharma, the friend of his devotees, and the destroyer of fear of death. The Stuti is brief (approximately 18-20 verses), profoundly emotional, and is celebrated as the model of antakala-smarana (remembrance at death). It is recited on Bhishma Ashtami, the day of his passing, and at the deathbeds of devout Vaishnavas. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi Bhishma Kruta Bhagavat Stuti; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Akrura Krta Vishnu Stotram and where is it found?
seoFaqs.answer: The Akrurakrta Vishnu Stotram appears in the Bhagavata Purana 10.40, when Akrura, the Yadava emissary sent to escort Krishna and Balarama to Mathura, halts to bathe in the Yamuna and witnesses an extraordinary darshan: he sees Krishna and Balarama not only on the chariot bank but also as Vishnu and Adi Shesha within the river waters. Overcome with devotion, Akrura recites a long stotram identifying Krishna with the supreme Vishnu, enumerating his cosmic functions, his avatars, and his presence within all beings. The hymn is held to be among the most theologically dense in the Bhagavata Purana and is recited by Vaishnavas seeking direct vision (sakshatkara) of the Lord. (Sources: StotraSamhita Akrurakrta Vishnu Stotram; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: What is distinctive about the Sanat Kumara Vishnu Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Sanat Kumara is the eldest of the four mind-born sons of Brahma (the Four Kumaras: Sanaka, Sanandana, Sanatana, Sanat Kumara) and is regarded as the eternal teen-age sage who taught the great rishis the highest mental and spiritual sciences. His Vishnu Stotram, preserved in the Padma Purana and quoted in the Mahabharata, is uttered in response to Sage Shukra's request to describe Vishnu's greatness. The hymn is distinctive for its philosophical density: Sanat Kumara begins by describing Vishnu as the creator and destroyer of all beings and proceeds through a systematic enumeration of Vishnu's relationship with sat-chit-ananda, the Vedas and the cosmos. It is recited by sannyasis and seekers of jnana-yoga more often than by family devotees. (Sources: Wikipedia Four Kumaras; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is the Vishnu Sahasranama itself often called simply Vishnu Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Vishnu Sahasranama, narrated by Bhishma on his death-bed at the request of Yudhishthira and recorded in the Anushasana Parva of the Mahabharata, contains 1000 names of Vishnu in 108 shlokas of Anushtubh meter. While its formal name highlights the thousand names (sahasranama), traditional usage commonly abbreviates the title to Vishnu Stotram in everyday speech, since it is the single most recited Vishnu praise-hymn in Hindu households. Adi Shankara wrote a complete bhashya (commentary) on it, and the Sahasranama is among the most thoroughly studied Vaishnava texts. When a Smarta or Vaishnava household says they recite Vishnu Stotram daily, they almost always mean the Sahasranama unless otherwise specified. (Sources: Shivalli Brahmins; Swami Krishnananda Vishnu Sahasranama; JKYog Vishnu Sahasranamam.)

seoFaqs.question: Which Vishnu Stotram is recommended for daily home recitation?
seoFaqs.answer: The traditional recommendation for daily Vaishnava household recitation is the Vishnu Sahasranama Stotram, which takes approximately 30 to 40 minutes at a measured pace. Households unable to commit that time often substitute the shorter Vishnu Ashtottara Shatanamavali (108 names, 5-7 minutes), the Vishnu Shatanama Stotram, or the Bhishma Stuti from Bhagavata Purana 1.9 (about 5-6 minutes). Thursday is the day of Vishnu and is preferred for the longer Sahasranama; daily morning recitation across the week typically uses the shorter forms. The Akrurakrta and Sanat Kumara Vishnu Stotrams are reserved for special contemplative practice rather than daily japa, due to their more philosophical content. (Sources: Swami Krishnananda; Shlokam Vishnu Shatanama Stotram; JKYog.)
